title: Tea From Nepal Nepal’s tea sector is gaining global attention through Himalayan origin, orthodox

slides.speaker_note: Introduce Nepal’s tea sector as an emerging premium origin with strong Himalayan identity. Highlight the focus on orthodox, organic, and traceable teas, then frame the presentation around 2026 production outlook, export growth, market positioning, and key opportunities.

content.__speaker_note__: Introduce Nepal’s tea sector as an emerging premium origin with strong Himalayan identity. Highlight the focus on orthodox, organic, and traceable teas, then frame the presentation around 2026 production outlook, export growth, market positioning, and key opportunities.

slides.speaker_note: This slide summarizes Nepal’s tea production outlook. The key message is that the sector is expanding steadily, but not rapidly. Production is projected to rise from 26,000 metric tons in 2021 to nearly 28,000 metric tons by 2026, supported by an average annual supply growth rate of 1.3% since 2017.

ui.description: Dark split-slide layout with a left title and chart area, right stacked detail cards, and aligned footer metadata.

components.description: Full canvas dark background layer that supports the high contrast white text and red chart palette.
Large headline area on the upper left with a short explanatory subtitle beneath it.
Three repeated detail rows on the right, each with an arrow icon, heading, body text, and separator line.

runs.text: Production is expected to reach nearly

Production is expected to reach nearly

components.description: Bottom footer band containing company label, date, and website aligned across the slide width.

left_intro_block.intro_caption: Nepal tea output shows **steady growth** toward 2026.

detail_row.row_body: Production is expected to reach nearly **28,000 metric tons** by 2026.

detail_row_2.row_body: Output stood at **26,000 metric tons** in 2021, setting the base for growth.

detail_row_3.row_body: Supply has risen at an average **1.3% per year** since 2017.

content.__speaker_note__: This slide summarizes Nepal’s tea production outlook. The key message is that the sector is expanding steadily, but not rapidly. Production is projected to rise from 26,000 metric tons in 2021 to nearly 28,000 metric tons by 2026, supported by an average annual supply growth rate of 1.3% since 2017.

slides.speaker_note: This slide highlights how Nepal’s tea industry can differentiate itself through organic production and premium positioning. Emphasize that the organic share supports wellness-focused consumer demand, while specialty origin and transparent sourcing help Nepal compete in higher-value tea segments.

ui.description: A clean split slide layout with large header text, three equal goal columns, and a tall right-side image panel.

runs.text: and a premium origin story, matching consumer demand for wellness, responsibility, and transparent sourcing.

, specialty origins, and higher-value beverage experiences.

components.description: Full-height photographic panel on the right side with a small decorative logo mark near the bottom.

intro_text_block.intro_paragraph: Nepal’s tea sector benefits from **organic credentials** and a premium origin story, matching consumer demand for wellness, responsibility, and transparent sourcing.

goal_items_grid.item_body: Around **70% of production** is described as organic, creating a strong base for health-conscious and environmentally aware consumers.
Nepal aligns with demand for **premium loose leaf tea**, specialty origins, and higher-value beverage experiences.
Transparent sourcing and responsible production strengthen credibility, helping Nepal stand out in global specialty tea markets.

content.__speaker_note__: This slide highlights how Nepal’s tea industry can differentiate itself through organic production and premium positioning. Emphasize that the organic share supports wellness-focused consumer demand, while specialty origin and transparent sourcing help Nepal compete in higher-value tea segments.

slides.speaker_note: Emphasize that Nepal tea should be positioned beyond commodity markets. Its key strengths are place-based identity, mountain-grown character, organic production, and specialty orthodox processing, all aligned with buyers seeking richer flavor and higher-value loose leaf tea.

intro_text_block.intro_paragraph: Nepal tea can stand out through **origin**, mountain character, organic methods, and premium loose leaf appeal.

content.__speaker_note__: Emphasize that Nepal tea should be positioned beyond commodity markets. Its key strengths are place-based identity, mountain-grown character, organic production, and specialty orthodox processing, all aligned with buyers seeking richer flavor and higher-value loose leaf tea.

slides.speaker_note: This slide highlights practical growth paths for Nepal’s tea sector. The main message is that global demand for artisanal and specialty teas creates room for Nepal to compete through premium positioning, stronger traceability, better packaging, organic credentials, and consistent quality rather than scale alone.

header_text_block.supporting_paragraph: Nepal can grow by expanding **premium export channels**, strengthening origin transparency, improving packaging, and promoting organic certification.

timeline_entry.entry_body: Expand **premium export channels** to reach buyers seeking distinctive Himalayan specialty teas.

timeline_entry_2.entry_body: Strengthen **origin transparency** through traceability, farm stories, and verified sourcing practices.

timeline_entry_3.entry_body: Improve **specialty packaging** to better signal quality, freshness, and premium positioning.

timeline_entry_4.entry_body: Promote **organic certification** and consistent quality to stand apart from larger producers.

content.__speaker_note__: This slide highlights practical growth paths for Nepal’s tea sector. The main message is that global demand for artisanal and specialty teas creates room for Nepal to compete through premium positioning, stronger traceability, better packaging, organic credentials, and consistent quality rather than scale alone.
